fileNamePrompt.title: Save file as
fileNamePrompt.description: Choose a file name. The file downloads to your browser's downloads folder.
fileNamePrompt.label: File name

newProject.basemapDescription: Choose an OpenFreeMap or Protomaps basemap, a blank background, or a MapLibre style URL.

common.pickColorFromScreen: Pick a color from the screen

xyz.label: Add XYZ Layer
xyz.description: Add a raster tile template using x, y, and z placeholders.

wms.label: Add WMS Layer
wms.description: Add a WMS GetMap service as a tiled raster layer.

wfs.label: Add WFS Layer
wfs.description: Add WFS features by requesting GeoJSON from a GetFeature service.

wmts.label: Add WMTS Layer
wmts.description: Add a WMTS tile URL template as a raster layer.

gpx.label: Add GPX Layer
gpx.description: Add GPX waypoints, routes, and tracks as one or more vector layers.

delimitedText.label: Add Delimited Text Layer
delimitedText.description: Add a delimited text file or URL as a point layer using longitude and latitude fields.

mbtiles.label: Add MBTiles Layer
mbtiles.description: Add a local MBTiles file as a raster or vector tile layer.

arcgis.label: Add ArcGIS Layer
arcgis.description: Add an ArcGIS FeatureServer layer, VectorTileServer layer, or portal item.

postgres.label: Add PostgreSQL Layer
postgres.description: Start Martin locally, discover PostGIS sources, and add a source as vector tiles.

deckglViz.label: Add Deck.gl Layer
deckglViz.description: Pick a deck.gl layer type, then load the example data or upload a CSV/JSON/GeoJSON file or URL.

video.label: Add Video Layer
video.description: Add a georeferenced video overlay by supplying an MP4 URL and four corner coordinates.

serviceLibrary.title: Saved services
serviceLibrary.empty: No saved services yet — fill in the form and choose “Save current”.
serviceLibrary.name: Name
serviceLibrary.errorName: Enter a name for the saved service.
serviceLibrary.errorTooLarge: File is too large to import (max 5 MB).
serviceLibrary.errorNoServices: No valid services found in that file.
serviceLibrary.errorRead: Could not read that file as a service library.

xyz.errorUrl: Enter an XYZ tile URL template.

wms.errorLayers: Enter one or more WMS layer names.

wfs.errorTypeName: Enter a WFS feature type name.
wfs.errorMaxFeaturesNumeric: Enter a numeric max features value.

wmts.errorUrl: Enter a WMTS tile URL template.

gpx.errorSelectType: Select at least one GPX layer type.
gpx.errorNotFound: The selected GPX layer types were not found.

delimitedText.errorFileMissing: Delimited text file data is missing.
delimitedText.readError: Could not read delimited text file.

mbtiles.errorSourceLayers: Enter at least one vector source layer.

postgres.statusNoSources: Martin is running, but no compatible PostGIS sources were found.
postgres.errorNoVectorLayers: The selected Martin source has no vector layers.
postgres.errorSelectSource: Select a Martin source to add.
postgres.desktopOnlyNotice: PostgreSQL layers are only available in GeoLibre Desktop. This feature runs a local Martin tile server, which the web app cannot launch.

video.errorHttps: Video URLs must start with https://.
video.errorCornerNumber: Enter a numeric {{corner}} longitude and latitude.
video.cornersNote: The four corners georeference the video on the map. The video must be served over HTTPS and the host must allow cross-origin requests (CORS) for the frames to render.

deckViz.errorChooseFile: Choose a CSV, JSON, or GeoJSON file.
deckViz.needsTabular: {{label}} needs tabular CSV/JSON data, not GeoJSON.
deckViz.errorExample: Could not load the example data.
deckViz.errorLoadFirst: Load the example data, or a file/URL, first.

offline.title: Download Offline Area
offline.description: Save the current map view's basemap tiles so this area keeps working without a network connection.
offline.noServiceWorker: Offline caching needs the installed web app. Tiles you download here won't be saved for offline use in this build.
offline.uncacheable: The active basemap ({{hosts}}) isn't on the offline-cacheable host list, so its tiles won't persist. Switch to an OpenFreeMap or CARTO basemap for offline use.
offline.tooManyTiles: This area exceeds the offline cache limit ({{max}} tiles); the oldest tiles may be evicted as newer ones download. Reduce the detail levels or pick a smaller area.

fieldCollection.title: Field Collection
fieldCollection.description: Capture point, line, or polygon observations against a custom form, placing geometry by GPS or by drawing on the map.
fieldCollection.noFields: No fields yet. Add fields to capture attributes, or create the layer to capture location-only points.
fieldCollection.captureHint: Use GPS or pick a point on the map to start a capture.
fieldCollection.drawHint: Draw the shape on the map to start a capture.
fieldCollection.dblClickHint: Tap to add vertices; double-click to finish.
fieldCollection.layerGone: The collection layer is no longer available.
fieldCollection.noGeolocation: Geolocation isn't available on this device.
fieldCollection.geolocationDenied: Couldn't get your location. Check location permissions and try again.
fieldCollection.photoTooLarge: That photo is too large (max {{max}}). Choose a smaller image.
fieldCollection.photoReadError: Couldn't read that image. Try another file.
fieldCollection.errorChoice: Choose one of the listed options.

georeferencer.title: Georeference Image
georeferencer.description: Pin an image to the map: load it, link image points to map locations, then add it as a georeferenced overlay.
georeferencer.loadImage: Click to load an image (PNG, JPG, TIFF)
georeferencer.imageTooLarge: That image is too large (max {{max}}). Choose a smaller image.
georeferencer.imageReadError: Couldn't read that image. Try another file.
georeferencer.imageNoDimensions: Couldn't determine the image size. Use a raster image (PNG, JPG, TIFF).
georeferencer.clickImageHint: Click a point on the image, then link it on the map.
georeferencer.gcpsReadError: Couldn't read control points from that file.
georeferencer.gcpsOutsideImage: Some control points fall outside the loaded image — they may be from a different image.
georeferencer.transformNeedsMore: This transform needs at least {{min}} control points.
georeferencer.needGcps: Add at least {{min}} control points to georeference.
georeferencer.collinear: Control points are collinear or coincident — move one off the line.
georeferencer.cornersOutOfRange: This fit places the image outside valid map bounds. Adjust the control points.
georeferencer.exportTiffHint: Warps the image to a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF in your browser (GDAL/WASM). The engine downloads on first use.
georeferencer.exporting: Exporting GeoTIFF… (loading GDAL on first use)
georeferencer.exportFailed: GeoTIFF export failed. See the console for details.

collaborate.title: Live collaboration
collaborate.description: Share a live session so others can see and edit this map with you in real time.
collaborate.connectFailed: Could not connect to the collaboration server.
collaborate.codeRequired: Enter a session code to join.

language.label: Language
language.description: Choose the language used for the interface.

legend.empty: No visible layers to include in the legend.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Configure project preferences and runtime settings.

menu.urlOverrideNote: URL layout parameters override saved settings.

map.constraintsDescription: Limits apply while the project is open.
map.errorBoundsUnavailable: The map bounds are not available yet.

layout.title: Layout
layout.showProjectInfoToolbar: Show project info in the toolbar
layout.urlParamsNote: URL layout parameters still apply when present in shared viewer links.

env.tokenDescription: Used by Project > Share to upload projects to share.geolibre.app. Create one under Settings > API tokens at <tokenLink>share.geolibre.app/settings</tokenLink>.
env.tokenStorageNote: Stored locally on this device and sent only to share.geolibre.app to authenticate uploads. On the web build it shares the same browser storage as other site data, so revoke it on share.geolibre.app if your machine is compromised.
env.secretsWarning: Values are stored in plain text in the project file and may be exposed when the project is shared. Avoid putting secrets here unless the project file stays private.
env.errorNamePattern: Environment variable names must start with a letter or underscore and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores.
env.stripPromptDesc: This project has {{count}} environment variable(s) (which may include API keys). They are stored in plain text in the project file and could be exposed if you share it. Remove them from the saved file? Your Settings keep them on this device either way.

geocoding.title: Geocoding
geocoding.description: Choose the geocoding backend used by Geocode Addresses and Reverse Geocode. Nominatim is the default and needs no key.
geocoding.requiresKey: This provider needs an API key or access token.
geocoding.optionalKey: The hosted service needs an API key; a self-hosted instance does not.
geocoding.noKey: This provider works without an API key.
geocoding.apiKeyPlaceholder: Paste the provider API key or token
geocoding.emailHint: Sent to identify the client to Nominatim, per its usage policy.
geocoding.secretsWarning: Keys are stored in plain text in the project file and may be exposed when the project is shared. They are also sent as query parameters, so they appear in geocoding request URLs visible in browser DevTools and any proxy logs.
geocoding.corsNote: Google does not officially allow browser requests to its Geocoding API, so requests may be blocked unless served through a same-origin proxy.

project.title: Project
project.description: Settings are saved with the `.geolibre.json` project.
project.name: Name

atmosphere.haloExtentHint: How far the atmospheric glow reaches past the globe. Lower keeps data tight to the surface; higher gives a wide, airy halo.

rasterTool.clientHint: Computes in your browser and adds the result to the map. For large rasters or geographic-CRS terrain, prefer the sidecar.
rasterTool.switchToClientHint: Or switch the engine to Client to run this tool in your browser.
rasterTool.useAddedLayer: Use a layer on the map
rasterTool.layerLoadError: Could not load the selected layer.
rasterTool.largeRasterWarning: Large raster ({{mp}} M pixels); the browser may be unresponsive while computing.
rasterTool.mapAddFailed: Map add failed (the result is still available to download): {{message}}
rasterTool.mapAddError: Could not add the result to the map.

item.directionsTooltip: Routing sends your waypoints to the public OSRM demo server (router.project-osrm.org).
item.openProjectFromUrlDesc: Load a public `.geolibre.json` project and add it to recent projects.
item.saveProjectAsDesc: Choose a file name. The project downloads to your browser's downloads folder.
item.directionsNoticeTitle: Directions uses a public routing server
item.directionsNoticeDesc: Turning on Directions sends the waypoints you place to the public OSRM demo server (router.project-osrm.org) to calculate routes — your coordinates leave your device for those requests. The demo server is rate-limited and supports driving only.
item.reverseGeocodeTooltip: Click the map to look up an address. The clicked coordinates are sent to the configured geocoding provider (public Nominatim by default; change it in Settings → Geocoding).
item.reverseGeocodeNoticeTitle: Reverse Geocode uses an external service
item.reverseGeocodeNoticeDesc: Turning on Reverse Geocode sends the coordinates of each point you click to the configured geocoding provider (the public Nominatim service by default) to resolve an address — your coordinates leave your device for those requests. You can choose a different provider in Settings → Geocoding.
item.networkNoticeTitle: Network analysis uses a public routing server
item.networkNoticeDesc: Running a Network analysis tool sends the coordinates of your input points to the public Valhalla routing server (valhalla1.openstreetmap.de) to compute isochrones and cost matrices — your coordinates leave your device for those requests. The public server is rate-limited; point it at your own server (Settings → Environment, VITE_ROUTING_ENDPOINT) for heavy use.
item.largeOsmPbfDesc: This file is about {{sizeMb}} MB. Parsing it converts every feature to GeoJSON in memory, which can be slow and may use a lot of memory in the browser. Continue?
item.largeVectorDesc: {{name}} has about {{count}} features. Loading it converts every feature to GeoJSON in memory and may slow down or freeze the app. Continue?
item.addOsmPbfLayerDesc: Load an OpenStreetMap .osm.pbf file. It is parsed in your browser and split into separate point, line, and polygon layers.
item.osmPbfUrlHint: Remote URLs load in the desktop app; in the browser the server must allow cross-origin requests (CORS).
item.loadingOsmPbfDesc: Parsing the file and converting features to GeoJSON. This runs in the background and may take a moment for large extracts.

error.invalidProjectUrl: Enter a valid HTTP or HTTPS project URL.
error.couldNotOpenRecentProject: Could not open the recent project.
error.couldNotLoadRecentProject: Could not load the recent project.
error.osmPbfNoFeatures: No point, line, or polygon features were found in this OSM PBF file.
error.couldNotLoadOsmPbf: Could not load the OSM PBF file.
error.osmPbfLoadFailedWithHint: {{message}} If this is a Mapbox Vector Tile (.pbf), it cannot be loaded as an OSM extract.
error.couldNotOpenOsmPbf: Could not open the OSM PBF file.
error.invalidOsmPbfUrl: Enter a valid http(s) URL to an .osm.pbf file.
error.couldNotDownloadOsmPbf: Could not download the OSM PBF file.
error.scenegraphInvalidLngLat: Enter a valid longitude and latitude.
error.scenegraphOutOfRange: Longitude must be between -180 and 180, and latitude between -90 and 90.
error.scenegraphNoPointFeatures: The GeoJSON has no point features to place models at.

description.postgis: Run PostGIS SQL against loaded layers. The PostGIS extension is loaded, so ST_* functions are available. The first run loads a ~19 MB database engine.
description.duckdb: Run DuckDB SQL against loaded layers, files, and URLs. The spatial extension is loaded, so ST_* functions are available. Cloud URLs (s3://, gs://, az://) are supported for public data.
description.sedona: Run Apache Sedona spatial SQL against loaded layers. Runs in-browser via CereusDB (a WebAssembly build of SedonaDB); on desktop it uses the SedonaDB sidecar when the optional sedona extra is installed. ST_* functions are available.

geocode.title: Geocode Addresses
geocode.description: Geocode a CSV of addresses into a point layer. Each matched row becomes a point carrying the original columns plus the resolved coordinates and match score.
geocode.noFile: Choose a CSV file to begin.
geocode.providerHint: Configure provider API keys and endpoints in Settings.
geocode.apiKeyRequired: {{provider}} needs an API key. Add one in Settings to get results.
geocode.corsNote: Google does not officially allow browser requests to its Geocoding API; requests may be blocked unless served through a same-origin proxy.
geocode.rowCapWarning: Only the first {{cap}} rows will be geocoded to respect the public Nominatim usage policy. Configure a self-hosted endpoint for larger batches.
geocode.skippedEmpty: Skipped {{count}} row(s) with an empty address.
geocode.summary: Geocoded {{matched}} of {{total}} row(s); {{failed}} had no match.

pythonConsole.title: Python Console
pythonConsole.placeholder: Python here · Ctrl/Cmd+Enter to run · Tab to autocomplete · ↑/↓ for history
pythonConsole.intro: Python console ready. Use the `geolibre` object to script the map, e.g. geolibre.get_center(). Tab autocompletes, ↑/↓ recall previous commands, and `await geolibre.load_package("numpy")` adds packages.
pythonConsole.loadFailed: Failed to load the Python runtime.
pythonConsole.discardChanges: Discard unsaved changes to the current script?
pythonConsole.editorPlaceholder: Write a multi-line Python script here. Ctrl/Cmd+Enter runs it (or the selection) in the console; Tab indents; Ctrl+Space autocompletes.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.empty: No widgets yet. Add a chart to summarize a layer.
dashboard.emptyNoLayers: Add a vector or DuckDB query layer to start building charts.
dashboard.noLayersHint: Add a vector or DuckDB query layer first.
dashboard.noData: This layer has no chartable attributes.

chart.emptyScatter: No rows have both fields set to a number.

editor.description: Choose a layer, a chart type, and the fields to plot.
editor.noLayers: No chartable layers. Add a vector or DuckDB query layer first.
editor.noFields: This layer has no numeric or categorical fields to chart.

assistant.title: AI Assistant
assistant.placeholder: Ask about your data, e.g. "show countries with population over 50 million" · Ctrl/Cmd+Enter to send
assistant.intro: Chat with your data. I can run Spatial SQL, add or remove layers, restyle them, and move the map — every change is undoable. Try "color the countries by population with a graduated red ramp".
assistant.needsKey: Configure an LLM provider in Settings → Environment to enable the assistant — an API key (GEMINI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY), AWS credentials for Bedrock, a local Ollama (OLLAMA_BASE_URL), or a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint (OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_BASE_URL).

storymap.title: Story Map
storymap.description: Build a scroll-driven story from map chapters. Each chapter captures a camera view and text.
storymap.empty: No chapters yet. Navigate the map, then add a chapter to capture the current view.

network.title: Network analysis
network.description: Isochrones, service areas, and origin–destination cost matrices from your point layers.
network.heading: Network
network.endpointNotice: Point coordinates are sent to the routing server ({{endpoint}}). The default is a shared public Valhalla server (FOSSGIS) with usage limits — point it at your own server (Settings → Environment, VITE_ROUTING_ENDPOINT) for heavy use.

statistics.title: Spatial statistics
statistics.description: Spatial autocorrelation, hotspot analysis, and density measures. Every tool runs client-side on your GeoJSON layers.
statistics.heading: Spatial Statistics

whitebox.runLocalHint: Run locally in WebAssembly (no Python sidecar)
whitebox.runningLocally: Running locally with WebAssembly. No sidecar required.

segmentation.title: AI Segmentation
segmentation.description: Turn imagery into vector features with SAM3 (segment-geospatial). Choose a GeoTIFF, describe what to segment, and get polygons back as a new layer.

error.enterPrompt: Enter a text prompt, e.g. “trees” or “buildings”.
error.sidecarUnreachable: Could not reach the GeoLibre sidecar.

layers.hiddenByGroup: Hidden because its group is not visible

rasterSymbology.customColorsValid: {{count}} colors, low to high. Use Reverse to flip.
rasterSymbology.customColorsHint: Enter at least two hex codes, separated by commas or spaces.
